content.claim: Around 85% of Hungarian students are in schools which can select students according to academic performance or recommendations from feeder schools, compared to the OECD average of 43%
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: Figure captures selective admission broadly (including standard 4-year gimnaziums and vocational tracks), not solely 6/8-year tracks
Does not establish causal impact on individual student outcomes
Benchmark comparison reflects policy design, not necessarily implementation effectiveness
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
